rows.definition: internal/sessionobs is the stdlib-only RSI scorecard that grades how far our own coding-session data has climbed the capture->structure->link->aggregate->learn ladder, the value-side complement to the cost-only tools/session_audit.py; its `fak sessions learn` rung is the registered loop consumer that reads the committed corpus.
rows.distinction: Not a live runtime Session: it is an offline analysis primitive measuring whether session transcripts are RSI-learnable, never a served or scheduled request, and its sessionsLearn consumer reads a committed corpus rather than driving a turn.

rows.definition: sessionobs.Corpus.SessionObsDebt is the headline integer the session-observability scorecard emits: the count of HARD ladder rungs (capture/structure/link/aggregate/learn) not yet built, retired only by building the real rung never by weakening a check.
rows.distinction: Not a Session's own budget: it is the deterministic debt COUNT over the whole session corpus's observability ladder, a scorecard verdict rather than a per-session runtime field.

rows.definition: taskscheduler is the `fak cron emit --target taskscheduler` OS-scheduler target that projects a registered fak loop into a Windows Task Scheduler `Register-ScheduledTask` snippet (alongside launchd and systemd), so a job survives reboot; the same family's NewNativeScheduler is an unrelated decode-engine constructor.
rows.distinction: Not the session.Scheduler (the DRIVE-state slot picker) nor the NativeScheduler engine loop: taskscheduler is an OS cron-emission target string, while modelengine.NewNativeScheduler is the constructor that builds a continuous-batching decode scheduler over a model.

rows.definition: TotalSessions, MultiTurnSessions and SingleTurnSessions are the cachevalueledger/cachevaluereport roll-up counters splitting the observed cache-value corpus by turn count, alongside the gateway's LiveSessions tally, the fleet's FleetSessions/tools snapshot, the resetHealth reaper bound maxResetHealthSessions, the dojo cross_session_warm_hit_rate posture metric, and the cachewitness honesty-fence SingleSessionMarginalX.
rows.distinction: Not the Session record itself nor ListSessions's live snapshot: these are aggregate session-population COUNTERS and ratios folded for cache-value/health reporting, not the per-session control state or its read query.

rows.definition: requireGLMDsaSession is a generated model-layer fence (sibling of requirePreNorm) asserting that a model.Session uses the GLM DSA attention path, recorded in the regenerated snapshot the harness commits.
rows.distinction: Not the gateway DRIVE-state session: it is a per-model-Session capability assertion guarding the GLM DSA forward path, not a run-state/budget control record.

rows.definition: agent.WithSessionGate is the RunOption that threads a live session gate plus its trace into the native agent turn loop (loop_session.go), so each turn is admitted/throttled and can drain operator steers at its boundary like a served request.
rows.distinction: Not WithSessionTable (which binds the DRIVE-state table itself): WithSessionGate wires the per-turn gate+trace seam in the native harness, and no production caller currently passes the table behind it.

rows.definition: FakSessionReset is a Windows Task Scheduler task-name label in the fak ops registration scripts, the reset sibling of the FakSessionCheckpoint scheduled task that keeps headless session state fresh.
rows.distinction: Not the in-table SessionReset directive: it is the OS scheduled-task NAME for the reset job, not the run-state clear-to-baseline control applied to a live session.

rows.definition: loop_session.go is the agent package's session-control seam for the turn loop: it threads the session gate (WithSessionGate/gateTurn), the operator steer splice (drainSteer), and per-tool-call routing into agent.Run's native loop.
rows.distinction: Not the runtime Session object: it is the source seam in internal/agent that BINDS session-control behavior into a turn loop, several of whose hooks have no production caller yet.

rows.definition: SessionType is the cachevaluereport per-observation workload label (run | guard | serve) that splits the cache-value roll-up, rendered as the SessionTypeMix histogram in the cachevalue review.
rows.distinction: Not the runtime Session nor its session.State: it is a coarse WORKLOAD-class tag on a cache-value observation row, used only for reporting breakdowns.

rows.definition: gateway.SlotFreed is the wire-neutral struct the scheduler's SlotEvent crosses into the gateway as (TraceID, Cause, Rev), projected by slotEventToSlotFreed and consumed by Server.ReclaimKVOnSlotFreed to drive real KV reclamation.
rows.distinction: Not the Slot signal itself nor the OnSlotFreed scheduler callback: SlotFreed is the gateway-boundary wire form of a freed slot, kept session-internals-blind so the gateway never imports internal/session.

rows.definition: FakSessionCheckpoint is the Windows Task Scheduler task name registered by tools/register_session_checkpoint.ps1 that runs the session-checkpoint job (the verified resumable packet sourced from witnessed state, #636) on a cadence.
rows.distinction: Not SessionImage (the loaded portable archive): the checkpoint task is the scheduled JOB that periodically writes a resume packet, not the persisted image format it produces.

rows.definition: The loop/bench witnessed run-status family: loopmgr.StatusWitnessRefused ("witness_refused") and StatusWitnessUnavailable ("witness_unavailable"), bench/loopgate VerdictWitnessed/OutcomeWitnessed ("witnessed") and its witnessed_done promotion, callavoid's WitnessedRedirect and its WitnessedEmpty count, and loopindex's WitnessedIndex health score.
rows.distinction: These are operational loop-run/coverage STATUS labels and counters, not the abi.WitnessOutcome enum the in-process gate folds: WitnessRefused/WitnessUnavailable are run-level dispositions, WitnessedRedirect/WitnessedEmpty are realized-fan-out evidence rows, and WitnessedIndex is a wired-stage health number.

rows.definition: The unwitnessed-claim deny/re-arm reason family: loopgate.ReasonDoneUnwitnessed ("LOOP_DONE_UNWITNESSED", the re-arm reason when a done claim has no witness, as adjudicated for a claim-unwitnessed turn) and the proposed context-safety SAFETY_UNWITNESSED DOS refusal token for a rendered number with no tamper-evident source.
rows.distinction: These name the 'a claim arrived with NO corroboration' case, distinct from abi WitnessRefuted (evidence actively contradicts): an unwitnessed claim re-arms or refuses for ABSENCE of proof, not for disproof.

rows.definition: modelroute.ReviewRefute is the scout-review verdict value "refute" returned when an opt-in pre-commit review rejects a diff; safecommit raises it to the ReasonReviewRefuted ("REVIEW_REFUTED") refusal that blocks the commit before it lands.
rows.distinction: Not a world-state Refutation of a cached witness: ReviewRefute is a reviewer's reject verdict on a proposed DIFF, and ReasonReviewRefuted is the commit-gate refusal it produces, neither touches the cache coherence bus.

rows.definition: supportmaturity.WitnessFor returns the exact non-author WitnessKind that must PROVE a given support-maturity rung, the binding the promotion check enforces (the evidence kind must be precisely the witness WitnessFor names).
rows.distinction: Not the WitnessResolver engine nor a generic claim: WitnessFor is a pure rung->required-witness-kind MAPPING in the support-maturity ladder, naming what evidence a promotion demands.

rows.definition: loopmgr.EventWitness is the loop-ledger event KIND carrying a run's external verdict (witnessed_done / witness_refused / witness_unavailable), the row that supplies the independent done-evidence the loopverify corpus folds beside each self-reported EventEnd.
rows.distinction: Not the WitnessOutcome verdict value it carries: EventWitness is the ledger EVENT type appended to the run journal, the transport for a verdict rather than the verdict enum itself.

rows.definition: internal/cachewitness is the observation seam that folds the WITNESSED kv_prefix metric family (KVPrefixWitness.ReuseRatio over fak_gateway_kv_prefix_reused_tokens_total) into a provenance-labeled Record, keeping WITNESSED reuse strictly distinct from OBSERVED provider cache_read and exposing the honesty-fence SingleSessionMarginalX.
rows.distinction: Not a world-state witness on the coherence bus: cachewitness measures realized KV-prefix REUSE value with WITNESSED/OBSERVED provenance discipline, never admitting or refuting a cache entry's correctness.

rows.definition: loopgate.WitnessResult is the witness adapter's evidence summary struct (Outcome, Reason, Detail, RawVerdict, Rung) that Adjudicate consumes to accept a done claim only on OutcomeWitnessed, parsed from the dos commit-audit/verify JSON.
rows.distinction: Not the WitnessOutcome enum it embeds nor the WitnessResolver that fills it: WitnessResult is the full adapter RESULT record carrying the outcome plus its evidence rung and detail.

rows.definition: syspromptmmu.witnessPrefix is the constant "blob-sha256:" that labels a syspromptmmu Witness string as a content-blob hash (not a git object id), so a system-prompt span's admitting witness is recognizable as a content digest.
rows.distinction: Not a World-state witness value itself: witnessPrefix is the fixed STRING TAG prepended to a content-hash witness, marking its kind rather than carrying world state.

rows.definition: TestPrefixReuseFanoutWitness is the cmd/fanbench soundness test that grounds the fan-out prefix-reuse GEOMETRY claim: it prefills the master-goal prefix once and proves N cloned sub-agents are bit-identical to an independent full prefill (max|delta|=0).
rows.distinction: Not a runtime witness verdict: it is a TEST-time correctness PROOF artifact for the fan-out KV-prefix reuse claim, not a kernel require-witness gate or a coherence-bus witness.

rows.definition: callavoid.TallyFromCountersWitnessed is TallyFromCounters with a set of WITNESSED productive denies (each a WitnessedRedirect carrying its enumerated variant set), netting witnessed denies so each deny is counted exactly once and only a non-forgeable, deduplicated fan-out is credited into the graded headline.
rows.distinction: Not a witness verdict: it is the FOLD that turns enumerated-variant deny evidence into a realized amplification tally, crediting nothing for an empty witness (WitnessedEmpty) so a claim can never inflate the score.
